stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 166.4 °C and a standard deviation of 10.7 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically underestimates the true value by between 3.3% and 5.8%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 166.4 °C. After correcting for the 4.5% bias: ≈ 174.2 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.3%-5.8%) and sampling variability: [168.2, 180.5]. Point estimate: 174.2 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
